conclusion: SIRT1 rs3758391/C and rs4746720/C were more common in centenarians than controls. Genotype distribution showed significant case-control difference.
After correcting for multiple testing, no individual SIRT1 SNPs were significantly associated with longevity. Gene-based analysis was suggestive.
No association was found between SIRT1 SNPs and longevity in the LonGenity cohort.
Carriers of the minor C allele of rs12778366 had better glucose tolerance and lower HbA1c in the Leiden Longevity Study offspring.
After correcting for multiple testing, no individual SIRT1 SNPs were significantly associated with longevity.
No association was found between SIRT1 SNPs and longevity in the LonGenity cohort.
SIRT1 rs7896005 had significant effects on telomere length and was associated with longevity-related telomere maintenance.
No association was found between SIRT1 SNPs and longevity in the LonGenity cohort.
SIRT1 rs4746720/C was more common in centenarians. Genotype distribution did not differ significantly when analyzed independently.
Longitudinal analysis showed an association for rs11555236 in whole-cohort survival analysis. Cross-sectional centenarian vs control comparison was not significant.
No significant results were observed for SIRT3 variants in the European candidate gene longevity study.
Longitudinal analysis showed an association for rs4980329 with survival. Cross-sectional centenarian comparison was not significant.
Gene-based analysis showed a significant association of SIRT6 variations with longevity. This was the strongest sirtuin signal in the multi-cohort study.
